notes: Exemplary work. All five geographic analyses present and accurate: centroids (36.99°N, 96.51°W weighted vs. 37.34°N, 96.48°W unweighted) correctly calculated; extremes correctly identified (Anchorage, Honolulu, Portland ME); latitude bands match exactly (40-45°N has 316 cities, 30-35°N has 38.58M population); east-west split accurate (546 cities / 67.0M pop east; 454 cities / 64.1M pop west); state spread analysis thorough with Texas correctly identified as most sprawling (15.59°). Insights are sophisticated and correct: explains population concentration in Sun Belt despite higher city density in Northeast/Midwest, discusses the significance of the two centroid positions, contextualizes Hawaii/Alaska geographic extremes, and clearly articulates the fewer-but-larger Western cities pattern. Report is well-organized with effective tables, clear section headers, and interpretive text. Minor: two identical output files and code artifacts shown, but these don't detract from core deliverable quality.
